titleStmt.title: Letter from Owen Foley and sent by the Inspector of Schools to the Chief Secretary's Office, 22 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Owen Foley

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter circulated among government officials in Dublin. It relates to the situation of children whose mother is often drunk/imprisoned and whose father serves in the army.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3730

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Enquiry re Mary A Curleys
Children CHIEF SECRETARY'S OFFICE
3730 29 Feb 1916
County of Wicklow
Bray 22. 2. 16 I beg to state the children of Mary
A Curley were not committed to
Industrial Schools. They were
in the Rathdown Union Work-
house and, in the interest of the
children, are boarded out in
Gorey District by the Rathdown
Guardians. The mother Mary A
Curley is a woman of drunken
habits, and very doubtful character,
and it is well for the children
that she should not get charge
of them, as if she does they
probably in the near future will
get into trouble. Her husband is at present in
the Army and her object in
seeking to get them; is to
get her separation allowance
increased, otherwise she would
not mind about them. This woman
most of her time is without
a home of her own and spends
a good deal of her time in
Prison for drunkenness and other
offences. Owen Foley Sq 52896
HC
Assist Under Secretary
with reference to your
minute of the 15th inst.
I beg to report that
Mrs Curley's children
are not detained in
Industrial Schools
signature Reply as above
adding that H. Go.
cannot interfere
with the Rathtown
Guardians in the
exercise of their
powers in the
matter.
29.2.16 done JGD 1/3
despd 2/3
REGISTERED 2 MAR 1916
Bray 23:2:16
Submitted
P. Mulligan
H 52267
D.2
Bray 23:2:16
For your information
Please see A.
Harry B Molony
The Inspr.
R & I Schools
